ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: I swear by the Day of Resurrection
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: I do swear by the Day of Judgment!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And I swear by the reproaching soul [to the certainty of resurrection].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And I do swear by the self-reproaching soul!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Does man think that We will not assemble his bones?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do people think We cannot reassemble their bones?

ayahs.textArabic: بَلَىٰ قَٰدِرِينَ عَلَىٰٓ أَن نُّسَوِّيَ بَنَانَهُۥ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Bala qadireena AAala an nusawwiya bananahu
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Yes. [We are] Able [even] to proportion his fingertips.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Yes ˹indeed˺! We are ˹most˺ capable of restoring ˹even˺ their very fingertips.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But man desires to continue in sin.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Still people want to deny what is yet to come,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He asks, "When is the Day of Resurrection?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: asking ˹mockingly˺, “When is this Day of Judgment?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So when vision is dazzled
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But when the sight is stunned,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the moon darkens
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the moon is dimmed,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the sun and the moon are joined,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the sun and the moon are brought together,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Man will say on that Day, "Where is the [place of] escape?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: on that Day one will cry, “Where is the escape?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! There is no refuge.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But no! There will be no refuge.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: To your Lord, that Day, is the [place of] permanence.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: On that Day all will end up before your Lord.

ayahs.textArabic: يُنَبَّؤُاْ ٱلۡإِنسَٰنُ يَوۡمَئِذِۭ بِمَا قَدَّمَ وَأَخَّرَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yunabbao alinsanu yawmaithin bima qaddama waakhkhara
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Man will be informed that Day of what he sent ahead and kept back.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: All will then be informed of what they have sent forth and left behind.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Rather, man, against himself, will be a witness,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In fact, people will testify against their own souls,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Even if he presents his excuses.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: despite the excuses they come up with.

ayahs.textArabic: لَا تُحَرِّكۡ بِهِۦ لِسَانَكَ لِتَعۡجَلَ بِهِۦٓ
ayahs.textTransliteration: La tuharrik bihi lisanaka litaAAjala bihi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Move not your tongue with it, [O Muhammad], to hasten with recitation of the Qur'an.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do not rush your tongue trying to memorize ˹a revelation of˺ the Quran.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, upon Us is its collection [in your heart] and [to make possible] its recitation.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It is certainly upon Us to ˹make you˺ memorize and recite it.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So when We have recited it [through Gabriel], then follow its recitation.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So once We have recited a revelation ˹through Gabriel˺, follow its recitation ˹closely˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then upon Us is its clarification [to you].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then it is surely upon Us to make it clear ˹to you˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! But you love the immediate
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But no! In fact, you love this fleeting world,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And leave the Hereafter.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and neglect the Hereafter.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [Some] faces, that Day, will be radiant,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: On that Day ˹some˺ faces will be bright,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Looking at their Lord.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: looking at their Lord.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [some] faces, that Day, will be contorted,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹other˺ faces will be gloomy,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Expecting that there will be done to them [something] backbreaking.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: anticipating something devastating to befall them.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! When the soul has reached the collar bones
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But no! ˹Beware of the day˺ when the soul reaches the collar bone ˹as it leaves˺,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it is said, "Who will cure [him]?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and it will be said, “Is there any healer ˹who can save this life˺?”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the dying one is certain that it is the [time of] separation
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And the dying person realizes it is ˹their˺ time to depart,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the leg is wound about the leg,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and ˹then˺ their feet are tied together ˹in a shroud˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: To your Lord, that Day, will be the procession.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: On that day they will be driven to your Lord ˹alone˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the disbeliever had not believed, nor had he prayed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: This denier neither believed nor prayed,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But [instead], he denied and turned away.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: but persisted in denial and turned away,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And then he went to his people, swaggering [in pride].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: then went to their own people, walking boastfully.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Woe to you, and woe!
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Woe to you, and more woe!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then woe to you, and woe!
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Again, woe to you, and even more woe!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Does man think that he will be left neglected?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Do people think they will be left without purpose?

ayahs.textArabic: أَلَمۡ يَكُ نُطۡفَةٗ مِّن مَّنِيّٖ يُمۡنَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Alam yaku nutfatan min manayyin yumna
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Had he not been a sperm from semen emitted?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Were they not ˹once˺ a sperm-drop emitted?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then he was a clinging clot, and [Allah] created [his form] and proportioned [him]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then they became a clinging clot ˹of blood˺, then He developed and perfected their form,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And made of him two mates, the male and the female.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: producing from it both sexes, male and female.

ayahs.textArabic: أَلَيۡسَ ذَٰلِكَ بِقَٰدِرٍ عَلَىٰٓ أَن يُحۡـِۧيَ ٱلۡمَوۡتَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Alaysa thalika biqadirin AAala an yuhyiya almawta
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Is not that [Creator] Able to give life to the dead?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Is such ˹a Creator˺ unable to bring the dead back to life?
